gerizim.label: Mount Gerizim
gerizim.note: UNESCO tentative list (ref. 5706), DMS E35 16 08 = 35.2689°E, rounded to 35.269°E

meru.label: Mount Meru
meru.note: Axis mundi of Hindu-Buddhist cosmography; Mt Meru, Tanzania, 36.75°E. Grid spacing 3° = 0.1 beru, same as Gerizim grid.

notable_anchors.note: Historical/archaeological anchors used in global comparison tables. All coordinates in decimal degrees East.

levant_landmarks.note: Key Levant sites used in local anchor-sweep comparison (34–37°E corridor).

beru.note: 1 beru = 30° of arc, attested in MUL.APIN (c. 1000 BCE). Linear value from Powell (1990) RlA Vol.7.

geodetic_dhanus.note: 56 × Thom Megalithic Yard = 46.4576 m; Geodetic Dhanus = 46.2494 m; deviation 0.45%

megalithic_yard.note: Thom MY = 0.8296 m (2.72 ft). Unit recovered statistically from stone circle diameters.

royal_cubit.note: Egyptian Royal Cubit = 0.5236 m (28 fingers of 18.7 mm each)

B.note: Baseline window for null rate calculation

null_rates.note: Geometric null rates: tier width / baseline window width

arc_test.note: Parameters for Framework 2 / 4 / 5 great-circle arc tests (PART XX).
arc_test.note_tolerance: ±0.01 beru absolute = ±107 m arc window (P₀=0.02). Derived from Powell rod ±0.3% mfg tolerance.

quantum_unit_test.note: Parameters for Framework 3 Thom-style quantum unit test.
quantum_unit_test.note_method: r = (dim / unit) mod 0.5 mapped to [0,0.25]. KS test against uniform null.

levant.note: Levant sweep range for anchor sensitivity analysis

global.note: Full-circle sweep for global anchor uniqueness audit

simulation.note: Parameters for permutation / bootstrap / KDE null models

keywords.note: All keyword lists used for site classification. Scripts must load these from config — no hardcoded keyword lists in analysis/ or lib/.

dome_forms.note: Morphological keywords for domed/spherical monumental architecture. 4 roots, 7 inflected forms. Unambiguous = always include; ambiguous = require architectural context sentence. Keywords chorten, dagoba, reliquary tower, hemispherical, cupola, rotunda were tested but removed: chorten/dagoba are valid synonyms but were not pre-specified; reliquary tower is a distinct form; hemispherical/cupola/rotunda overlap with context-validation patterns and inflate the corpus beyond the pre-registered design.

founding_capital.note: Unambiguous = always a founding capital. Ambiguous = require sentence-level context confirming a political entity (kingdom, empire, state). 'founded in' removed — too broad (matches any old city).

sacred_origin.note: Unambiguous = inherently sacred/religious. Ambiguous = require sentence-level context confirming a religious tradition, deity, or sacred practice rather than a secular idea, person, or art movement.

founding_monument.note: Unambiguous = unequivocally the first/oldest of a type. Ambiguous = require sentence-level context confirming a built monument that originated or defined a tradition. Dropped: 'masterpiece of', 'pioneering', 'finest example', 'most outstanding example', 'supreme example' — these are generic UNESCO praise applied to any impressive site, not evidence of founding/prototype status.

founding_axis.note: Unambiguous = a named major route/fortification system. Ambiguous = require sentence-level context confirming a major transcontinental route or imperial frontier. regex_unambiguous = regex patterns that are accepted without context validation.

ancient_landscape.note: Unambiguous = explicitly ancient/continuous. Ambiguous = require sentence-level context confirming antiquity or long-term continuity (millennia, centuries, traditional agriculture), not a modern designed landscape.

buddhist_heritage.note: Regex patterns (case-insensitive) for UNESCO Buddhist Heritage thematic scope.
